purpose: Sourced candidate list for Ising machines as hardware-combinatorial / combinatorial-machine comparators under PG1-PG8 physical-regime source-intake criteria. This queues Ising-machine classes for later source review, without treating combinatorial optimization as Formation Medium substrate evidence.

critical_review_prework.hard_bottleneck_attacked: whether physical combinatorial settling / Ising ground-state search is a more relevant comparator than S-matrix observables for Formation Medium, while still avoiding ordinary optimization-hardware drift
thin_stick_risks: Ising ground-state search treated as native Formation Medium settling law
combinatorial optimization hardware treated as substrate evidence
benchmark performance treated as physical-regime validation
programmed couplings treated as native admissibility
critical_review_prework.negative_progress_risk: Could become a hardware-optimization shopping list unless it stays a comparator track with kill conditions and no substrate promotion.
critical_review_prework.why_not_convenient_drift: It corrects the physical-regime comparator map by adding a missing combinatorial-machine layer distinct from S-matrix, Route A, CRBSM, and high-resonance.

anti_flattening_rule: Ising machines are hardware-combinatorial / combinatorial-machine comparators. They are not S-matrix, not high-resonance bridge by default, not Route A, not CRBSM, not Formation Medium, not selected substrate, and not native settling law by default.

candidate_classes.label: Ising-machine review / taxonomy sources
candidate_classes.why_candidate: General Ising-machine reviews define the field as hardware solvers for Ising ground states and combinatorial optimization, giving the comparator its correct layer.
kill_notes: review taxonomy is not source extraction
hardware solver framing is not substrate evidence
ground-state success probability is not Formation Medium readout
performance comparison is not native settling proof

candidate_classes.label: coherent optical Ising machines / optical parametric oscillator networks
candidate_classes.why_candidate: Coherent Ising machines give a physical oscillator network whose phases encode Ising spins and settle toward low-energy configurations for combinatorial optimization.
kill_notes: OPO phase/spin encoding is not canonical positive-geometric readout
measurement-feedback couplings can be external control
Max-Cut benchmark success is not Formation Medium evidence
optical pulse identity may be instrument-defined

candidate_classes.label: CMOS annealing / digital annealer Ising machines
candidate_classes.why_candidate: CMOS and digital annealing systems show scalable hardware mapping from combinatorial problems to Ising objectives, useful as negative controls for ordinary optimization hardware.
kill_notes: digital/CMOS annealing is likely ordinary optimization hardware
programmable couplings are not native admissibility
spin assignments are not Formation Medium readout
useful mostly as negative-control comparator

candidate_classes.label: probabilistic spintronic / p-bit / magnetic tunnel junction Ising machines
candidate_classes.why_candidate: Probabilistic p-bit and magnetic tunnel junction systems supply stochastic physical bits and hardware-native randomness for Ising-machine dynamics, making them relevant to physical settling/combinatorics comparison.
kill_notes: probabilistic bits are not Formation Medium carriers by default
entropy source is not native readout
annealing schedule or feedback may be externally imposed
optimization result is not canonical readout

candidate_classes.label: quantum annealing / superconducting artificial spin systems
candidate_classes.why_candidate: Quantum annealing systems physically implement programmable interacting-spin Hamiltonians and ground-state search, giving a strong comparator for physical optimization versus Formation Medium settling.
kill_notes: programmable spin-spin couplings are not intrinsic Formation Medium admissibility
quantum annealing schedule is not native settling law
ground-state readout is conventional optimization readout
quantum hardware status is not substrate selection

source_candidates.title: Ising machines as hardware solvers of combinatorial optimization problems
source_candidates.track_relevance: review/taxonomy of Ising machines as hardware solvers for Ising ground states and combinatorial optimization
source_candidates.candidate_value: anchors Ising machines as a distinct hardware-combinatorial comparator class
source_candidates.blocked_inference: review framing is not Formation Medium substrate evidence

source_candidates.title: A coherent Ising machine for 2000-node optimization problems
source_candidates.track_relevance: large coherent optical Ising machine for mapped combinatorial optimization problems
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests physical oscillator-network settling toward Ising objective minima
source_candidates.blocked_inference: coherent Ising optimization is not native Formation Medium settling law

source_candidates.title: A fully programmable 100-spin coherent Ising machine with all-to-all connections
source_candidates.track_relevance: programmable OPO coherent Ising machine with all-to-all couplings
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests programmable coupling/readout as a negative-control boundary for physical combinatorial settling
source_candidates.blocked_inference: all-to-all programmability is not native admissibility

source_candidates.title: A poor man's coherent Ising machine based on opto-electronic feedback systems for solving optimization problems
source_candidates.track_relevance: opto-electronic feedback implementation of coherent Ising machine dynamics
source_candidates.candidate_value: useful negative control for feedback-driven physical optimization versus intrinsic dynamics
source_candidates.blocked_inference: opto-electronic feedback is not intrinsic Formation Medium readout

source_candidates.title: A 20k-Spin Ising Chip to Solve Combinatorial Optimization Problems With CMOS Annealing
source_candidates.track_relevance: large CMOS annealing Ising chip for combinatorial optimization
source_candidates.candidate_value: strong hardware-negative-control candidate for ordinary programmed optimization
source_candidates.blocked_inference: CMOS annealing chip is not Formation Medium substrate evidence

source_candidates.title: Physics-inspired optimization for quadratic unconstrained problems using a digital annealer
source_candidates.candidate_value: clarifies difference between physics-inspired optimization and physical native settling
source_candidates.blocked_inference: digital annealer is not physical Formation Medium settling

source_candidates.title: Massively parallel probabilistic computing with sparse Ising machines
source_candidates.track_relevance: probabilistic computing / p-bit sparse Ising machine platform
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests stochastic physical-bit settling and sparse combinatorial dynamics
source_candidates.blocked_inference: p-bit sampling is not canonical Formation Medium readout

source_candidates.title: An integrated-circuit-based probabilistic computer that uses voltage-controlled magnetic tunnel junctions as its entropy source
source_candidates.track_relevance: integrated probabilistic computer using VC-MTJs as entropy source for probabilistic Ising-machine logic
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests hardware-native entropy and probabilistic-bit carrier status
source_candidates.blocked_inference: VC-MTJ entropy source is not native Formation Medium readout

source_candidates.title: Intrinsic annealing in a hybrid memristor-magnetic tunnel junction Ising machine
source_candidates.track_relevance: hybrid memristor-MTJ Ising machine with intrinsic annealing claim
source_candidates.candidate_value: high-priority future review for whether annealing is genuinely intrinsic or feedback/control-dependent
source_candidates.blocked_inference: intrinsic annealing claim is not Formation Medium settling-law proof

source_candidates.title: Quantum annealing with manufactured spins
source_candidates.track_relevance: superconducting artificial spin system using quantum annealing for programmable Ising ground-state search
source_candidates.candidate_value: quantum-annealing comparator for physical ground-state search versus Formation Medium settling
source_candidates.blocked_inference: programmable quantum annealing is not substrate selection

next_action_policy.preferred_next: keep SN-C3 as the next scattering-native physical-observable review, while scheduling PRI-005 as an Ising-machine comparator queue; if choosing Ising next, start with IM-C3 probabilistic spintronic/p-bit systems or IM-C1 coherent optical Ising machines
do_not_do_next: declare Ising machines closer than S-matrix by assertion
treat combinatorial optimization performance as Formation Medium evidence
move sources into SOURCE_EXTRACTED_CLAIMS without ingestion and extraction
treat Ising ground state as canonical positive-geometric readout
treat annealing schedule as native settling law

non_promotion_rule: This PRI-005 artifact is a sourced candidate list only. It does not ingest sources into the repo corpus, extract source claims, validate Ising machines, beat S-matrix comparison, validate high-resonance, validate Route A, validate CRBSM, select a substrate, prove a settling law, or establish engineering readiness.
